title: Run Tiny Experiments
subtitle: A small starting shape for work where personal, time-bounded experiments need an explicit hypothesis, window, and persist/pause/pivot closure.
scope: A small set of running and recently-closed pacts, treated as the unit of learning rather than the unit of finishing.
sourceCoverage: Template only; replace example experiments with your own pacts.

templateMeta.useWhen: Use this when you want to run small, time-bounded experiments on yourself or a project — and need a place to write down the hypothesis, the window, and the persist/pause/pivot decision at the end.
goodFor: research probes that need a window
anything you want to learn from rather than finish
templateMeta.operatingThesis: Treat each card as a pact: a hypothesis, a window with a real end date, and a closure that picks one of three legitimate moves — persist, pause, or pivot. The closure carries the data; running is just the wait.
notFor: open-ended task lists with no question being asked
post-hoc journaling with no prior bet

flowchart LR status_snapshot["status-snapshot

semanticDefinition.objectiveRole: Make time-bounded pacts operational by separating hypotheses, signals, attempts, and closure decisions.
semanticDefinition.templateSubtitle: A small starting shape for work where personal, time-bounded experiments need an explicit hypothesis, window, and persist/pause/pivot closure.
semanticDefinition.templateScope: A small set of running and recently-closed pacts, treated as the unit of learning rather than the unit of finishing.
semanticDefinition.templateObjective: Make the experiments you are actually running visible — their hypotheses, their windows, and the closure decision waiting at the end of each.
semanticDefinition.templateSuccessCondition: Every running pact has an end date and at least one trackable signal. Every closed pact has an outcome (persist / pause / pivot) and a one-or-two-sentence rationale.

sections.title: Status Snapshot
sections.role: Summarize experiment pressure without carrying pact details.
sections.rationale: This section keeps current experiment pressure visible while pact hypotheses and attempts stay inspectable.

sections.title: Tiny Experiments
sections.role: Track each pact with hypothesis, window, signals, and closure state.
sections.rationale: Each experiment is a pact: hypothesis, time window, signals, and closure decision are the unit of learning.

sections.title: Attempts
sections.role: Record actions tried during or around the experiments and what they proved.
sections.rationale: Attempts preserve what was tried so pact outcomes are grounded in behavior rather than memory.

attempt-log.name: Attempt Log
attempt-log.description: A record of actions taken against a problem and what each one proved. Distinct from findings (observations) and decisions (chosen directions).
attempt-log.structuralContract: Two-column card grid. Each card is one attempt with an outcome. Status is attempt-log-status. Use when capturing tried fixes, probes, and shipped workarounds — not to record decisions or generic findings.
notFor: positions in an ongoing debate (use maintainer-stance)

promptGuidance.operatingThesis: Treat each card as an action with a result. Every attempt must name what was tried and what it proved; shipped attempts must link their shipping site.
inspect: Verify each attempt has a concrete outcome — probes that revealed nothing are noise.
Check the shipped_in URL resolves and the referenced code or patch still exists.
update: When an attempt is superseded by a newer one, mark it superseded rather than deleting.
Preserve rejected attempts — they tell the next reader what not to try.
avoid: Do not collapse attempts into observations or decisions.
Do not mix generic notes into attempt cards — supporting context goes in notes[].

aiActions.name: Propose supersession
aiActions.description: If a newer attempt productionised the same insight, suggest marking this card superseded and linking the newer shipping site.

aiActions.name: Find shipping commit
aiActions.description: Search the referenced repos for the commit that productionised this attempt; fill shipped_in if missing.

status-snapshot.name: Status Snapshot
status-snapshot.description: A summary section that compresses the current document into top-level counts and distribution signals.
status-snapshot.structuralContract: Stats-first section with snapshot cards and no item grid requirement. Use for high-level totals and state distribution, not for individual entities.

promptGuidance.operatingThesis: Treat the section as document-wide compression, not as an item inventory.
inspect: Read the rest of the document before changing snapshot stats.
update: Recompute stats and callout language from the current section set and payload.
avoid: Do not add individual work items here.

status-snapshot.icon: <rect x='4' y='14' width='3' height='6' rx='1' opacity='.4'/><rect x='10.5' y='9' width='3' height='11' rx='1' opacity='.65'/><rect x='17' y='5' width='3' height='15' rx='1'/>

tiny-experiment.name: Tiny Experiment
tiny-experiment.description: A small, time-bounded experiment with an explicit hypothesis and a closure decision of persist, pause, or pivot. The unit of pact-shaped learning.
tiny-experiment.structuralContract: Two-column card grid. Each card is one experiment with a hypothesis, a window, the signals it watches, and on closure an outcome with rationale. Status is pact-status; outcome is pact-outcome and is only set when status is closed.
notFor: ongoing tasks without a hypothesis or window (use attempt-log)
cohort views across multiple experiments (use experiment-evidence-surface)
settled decisions with no live signal (use decision-record)

promptGuidance.operatingThesis: Each card is a pact: a declarative hypothesis, a window with start and end, the signals being tracked, and a closure that picks one of three legitimate moves — persist, pause, or pivot. The closure carries the data; the running state is just the wait.
inspect: Check that every running card has a window end date — undated experiments quietly turn into permanent commitments.
Check that closed cards have an outcome and a one or two sentence rationale.
If a card has been running past its window without a closure, that is itself a signal — either close it or extend the window deliberately.
update: When the window closes, set status=closed, set outcome, and write the rationale before moving on. Skipping the rationale loses the data the experiment was trying to gather.
If pivoting, link nextPact to the follow-up experiment so the chain stays traceable.
Persisting does not mean unchanged — write down what is being kept and what (if anything) is being adjusted.
avoid: Do not use this for tasks that have no hypothesis. Tasks without a question being asked belong in attempt-log or operating-surface.
Do not collapse persist/pause/pivot into pass/fail — the three-way choice is the point.
Do not let cards drift past their window without a closure. The closure is what makes it an experiment.

aiActions.name: Check window elapsed
aiActions.description: If the window end has passed and status is still running, propose closure — surface the signals collected so far and ask the user for an outcome and rationale.

aiActions.name: Draft closure rationale
aiActions.description: Read the signals and any linked attempts, then propose a one or two sentence rationale for the chosen outcome. Author still confirms.

relationships.rationale: Experiment status should summarize actual running or recently closed pacts.
Pact closure should be informed by what was tried and what it proved.

stageSignals.question: What first pact, hypothesis, window, and signal should be tracked?

stageSignals.when: tiny experiments exist but attempt-log has no attempts
stageSignals.question: What action has been tried, and what did it prove for the pact?
Which pacts should persist, pause, or pivot based on the tracked signals?

validOperations.label: Add experiment pact
validOperations.description: Add a tiny experiment with hypothesis, window, and signals.

validOperations.label: Record experiment attempt
validOperations.description: Record what was tried and what it proved for the pact.

validOperations.label: Close experiment pact
validOperations.description: Set an outcome and rationale for a pact at the end of its window.

objective: Make the experiments you are actually running visible — their hypotheses, their windows, and the closure decision waiting at the end of each.
successCondition: Every running pact has an end date and at least one trackable signal. Every closed pact has an outcome (persist / pause / pivot) and a one-or-two-sentence rationale.

callouts.title: How to use this starter
items: Keep cards small. A pact you cannot summarise as 'if I do X for N, then Y' is too big.
Set a real window. Undated experiments quietly turn into permanent commitments.
Close the loop. Persist, pause, or pivot — never silently drop. The closure is the experiment.

callouts.title: Inspired by
items: Anne-Laure Le Cunff, Tiny Experiments: How to Live Freely in a Goal-Obsessed World (2025).
PACT (Purposeful, Actionable, Continuous, Trackable) replaces SMART. Persist / pause / pivot replaces pass/fail.

sections.title: Status Snapshot

callout.title: Starter intent
items: Use the snapshot to make the running/closed split visible at a glance.
Let the section below carry the actual experiments.

sections.rationale: This section keeps current experiment pressure visible while pact hypotheses and attempts stay inspectable.

sections.title: Tiny Experiments

callout.title: How to read this section
items: Each card is one pact. Running cards still have their window open; closed cards carry their outcome.
If a running card's window has passed, that is itself a signal — close it or extend deliberately.

data.name: Act/surface split sharpens the registry

hypothesis.text: If I add a required kind: act | surface to every convergence type and update /convergence-advisor to ask the kind question first, new types entering the registry will declare kind cleanly and the advisor's first dialog turn will sharpen.

signals.text: Number of new types proposed via the advisor in the next two weeks.
Whether each new proposal cleanly answers act vs surface in its first turn.
Subjective sharpness when adding sections to existing docs.

notes: This is the inaugural pact for this template — the act/surface split itself, retro-fitted as a tiny experiment.
Closure window is 2026-05-14. If no new types come through the advisor in the window, the signal is silence — extend by one window or close as 'inconclusive, pause'.

data.name: Daily 10-minute living-doc pulse

hypothesis.text: If I read one living doc end-to-end every morning for 14 days, drift catches will increase and recall in working sessions will sharpen.

signals.text: Drift catches per week — cards discovered to be stale.
Subjective recall when entering a session cold.

rationale.text: Drift catches went from ~1/week to ~4/week; cold-session recall measurably faster. Persisting as standing morning practice.

notes: Example of a clean persist outcome — the practice produced what the hypothesis predicted.
Persisting does not mean unchanged: dropped the doc-rotation rule (was reading the next-due doc; now reading whichever feels stalest).

data.name: Auto-generate dossier prose from living-doc deltas

hypothesis.text: If I draft each period dossier from the change-log plus current living-doc state, prose quality will match hand-written within three cycles.

signals.text: Editorial pass count needed before publish.
Subjective tone match against hand-written reference dossiers.

rationale.text: Quality close on monitoring dossiers (3-4 edit passes) but tone fell flat on argumentative pieces (8+ passes). Pivoting to a hand-AI loop where the AI drafts only the structural skeleton.

notes: Example of a pivot outcome — the question got answered, but the answer redirected the work.
Pivoting created a new pact (hand-ai-dossier-loop) that would live in this same section once it's running.

sections.rationale: Each experiment is a pact: hypothesis, time window, signals, and closure decision are the unit of learning.

sections.title: Attempts

callout.title: Where attempts live
items: Day-to-day actions inside an experiment go here, not on the experiment card.
Link an attempt back to the experiment it serves with a brief note.

data.name: Updated /convergence-advisor to ask kind first

what_tried.text: Restructured step 3 of the skill to lead with 'is each card a unit of thinking-action or a projection of state?' before asking about sources/projection/derivation.

what_proved.text: Skill landed; first observation window is open. Serves the act-surface-split experiment above.

data.cost_to_apply: Two edits, one in .claude and one in .agents.
notes: This card belongs to the act-surface-split experiment — it's one of the actions inside that pact, not the pact itself.

sections.rationale: Attempts preserve what was tried so pact outcomes are grounded in behavior rather than memory.
